steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Identify the linear scale factor and apply the area scaling rule
steps.content: Each side of EFGH is 6 times the length of the corresponding side of ABCD, so the linear scale factor is $k = 6$. For similar figures, when linear dimensions scale by a factor of $k$, the area scales by $k^2$. Therefore, the area of EFGH is $6^2 = 36$ times the area of ABCD.

steps.title: Calculate the area of EFGH
steps.content: The area of ABCD is 90 square units. Since the area scales by a factor of 36, the area of EFGH is $90 \times 36 = 3,240$ square units. D is correct.
